scene_title: A court within the castle of the Earl of Gloucester

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Save thee, Curan.

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: And you, sir. I have been with your father, and given him

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: notice that the Duke of Cornwall and Regan his duchess will be

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: here with him this night.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: How comes that?

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: Nay, I know not. You have heard of the news abroad; I

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: mean the whispered ones, for they are yet but ear-kissing arguments?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Not I: pray you, what are they?

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: Have you heard of no likely wars toward, ’twixt the Dukes of Cornwall and Albany?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Not a word.

lines.speaker: CURAN
lines.text: You may do, then, in time. Fare you well, sir.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: The duke be here to-night? The better! best!

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: This weaves itself perforce into my business.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: My father hath set guard to take my brother;

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: And I have one thing, of a queasy question,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Which I must act: briefness and fortune, work!

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Brother, a word; descend: brother, I say!

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I am sure on’t, not a word.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I hear my father coming: pardon me:

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: In cunning I must draw my sword upon you

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Draw; seem to defend yourself; now quit you well.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Yield: come before my father. Light, ho, here!

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Fly, brother. Torches, torches! So, farewell.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Some blood drawn on me would beget opinion. Wounds his arm.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Of my more fierce endeavour: I have seen drunkards

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Do more than this in sport. Father, father!

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Stop, stop! No help?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Now, Edmund, where’s the villain?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Here stood he in the dark, his sharp sword out,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Mumbling of wicked charms, conjuring the moon

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: To stand auspicious mistress—

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: But where is he?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Look, sir, I bleed.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Where is the villain, Edmund?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Fled this way, sir. When by no means he could—

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Pursue him, ho! Go after. By no means what?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Persuade me to the murder of your lordship;

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: But that I told him, the revenging gods

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: ’Gainst parricides did all their thunders bend;

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Spoke, with how manifold and strong a bond

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: The child was bound to the father; sir, in fine,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Seeing how loathly opposite I stood

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: To his unnatural purpose, in fell motion,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: With his prepared sword, he charges home

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: My unprovided body, lanced mine arm:

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: But when he saw my best alarum’d spirits,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Bold in the quarrel’s right, roused to the encounter,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Or whether gasted by the noise I made,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Full suddenly he fled.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Let him fly far:

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Not in this land shall he remain uncaught;

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: And found—dispatch. The noble duke my master,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: My worthy arch and patron, comes to-night:

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: By his authority I will proclaim it,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: That he which finds him shall deserve our thanks,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Bringing the murderous coward to the stake;

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: He that conceals him, death.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: When I dissuaded him from his intent,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: And found him pight to do it, with curst speech

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I threaten’d to discover him: he replied,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: “Thou unpossessing bastard! dost thou think,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: If I would stand against thee, would the reposal

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Of any trust, virtue, or worth in thee

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Make thy words faith’d? No: what I should deny—

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: As this I would: ay, though thou didst produce

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: My very character—I’ld turn it all

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: To thy suggestion, plot, and damned practise:

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: And thou must make a dullard of the world,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: If they not thought the profits of my death

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Were very pregnant and potential spurs

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: To make thee seek it.”

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Strong and fasten’d villain

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Would he deny his letter? I never got him. Tucket within.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Hark, the duke’s trumpets! I know not why he comes.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: All ports I’ll bar; the villain shall not ’scape;

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: The duke must grant me that: besides, his picture

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I will send far and near, that all the kingdom

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: May have the due note of him; and of my land,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Loyal and natural boy, I’ll work the means

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: To make thee capable.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: How now, my noble friend! since I came hither,

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Which I can call but now, I have heard strange news.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: If it be true, all vengeance comes too short

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Which can pursue the offender. How dost, my lord?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: O, madam, my old heart is crack’d, it’s crack’d!

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: What, did my father’s godson seek your life?

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: He whom my father named? your Edgar?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: O, lady, lady, shame would have it hid!

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Was he not companion with the riotous knights

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: That tend upon my father?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I know not, madam: ’tis too bad, too bad.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Yes, madam, he was of that consort.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: No marvel, then, though he were ill affected:

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: ’Tis they have put him on the old man’s death,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: To have the expense and waste of his revenues.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: I have this present evening from my sister

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Been well inform’d of them; and with such cautions,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: That if they come to sojourn at my house,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: I’ll not be there.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Nor I, assure thee, Regan.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Edmund, I hear that you have shown your father

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: A child-like office.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: ’Twas my duty, sir.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: He did bewray his practise; and received

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: This hurt you see, striving to apprehend him.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Is he pursued?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Ay, my good lord.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: If he be taken, he shall never more

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Be fear’d of doing harm: make your own purpose,

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: How in my strength you please. For you, Edmund,

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Whose virtue and obedience doth this instant

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: So much commend itself, you shall be ours:

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: Natures of such deep trust we shall much need;

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: You we first seize on.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I shall serve you, sir,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Truly, however else.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: For him I thank your grace.

lines.speaker: CORNWALL
lines.text: You know not why we came to visit you—

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Thus out of season, threading dark-eyed night:

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Occasions, noble Gloucester, of some poise,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Wherein we must have use of your advice:

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Our father he hath writ, so hath our sister,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Of differences, which I least thought it fit

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: To answer from our home; the several messengers

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: From hence attend dispatch. Our good old friend,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Lay comforts to your bosom; and bestow

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Your needful counsel to our business,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Which craves the instant use.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I serve you, madam:

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Your graces are right welcome.

stage_directions.text: Enter Edmund, and Curan meets him.

My father watches: O sir, fly this place; Intelligence is given where you are hid; You have now the good advantage of the night: Have you not spoken ’gainst the Duke of Cornwall? He’s coming hither: now, i’ the night, i’ the haste, And Regan with him: have you nothing said Upon his party ’gainst the Duke of Albany? Advise yourself.
Enter Gloucester, and Servants with torches.

glosses.definition: probable conflicts — between Cornwall and Albany
propertyless illegitimate — Edmund's false quotation of Edgar
my worthy chief — Edmund greeting Cornwall

annotations.title: Edmund's deception of Gloucester — the subplot mirrors the main plot
annotations.body: Edmund's theatrical self-wounding and instant framing of Edgar mirrors in miniature what the sisters are doing to Lear: a child exploits a parent's credulity, uses the rhetoric of filial piety, and drives the parent into immediate disinheritance of the other child. Gloucester disinheriting Edgar in 2.1 is the dramatic echo of Lear disinheriting Cordelia in 1.1. This parallelism is what makes Lear structurally unusual — a secondary plot that does not decorate the main plot but philosophically interrogates it. The two plots are two experiments testing the same question: can parental love survive the failure of its own perception?
sources: William R. Elton, King Lear and the Gods (1966)

annotations.title: Edmund's fake wound — theater as evidence
annotations.body: Edmund cuts his own arm to fabricate evidence that Edgar attacked him. The gesture anticipates Iago's way of manufacturing proofs from material fragments: a wound is more persuasive than a story. Gloucester accepts the evidence instantly and disinherits Edgar in a single line. The scene is Shakespeare's compressed lesson that physical signs can lie more fluently than language. It is also the Gloucester subplot's duplicate of Lear's 1.1 mistake: a parent mis-reading a staged performance as truth. Edmund, like the sisters, has learned that theater is more convincing than life.
sources: Jonathan Bate, Soul of the Age (2008)

trials.prompt: How does Edmund's framing of Edgar in 2.1 relate structurally to 1.1?
options: 2.1 is the Gloucester-plot mirror of 1.1 — a parent disinheriting a true child on the word of a false one; the two plots run in deliberate parallel
Gloucester already knows Edgar is innocent

trials.prompt: Edmund cutting his own arm to frame Edgar parallels what earlier moment?
options: Lear's 1.1 mis-reading of staged filial performance as truth — Gloucester too accepts a staged appearance as evidence; the two plots mirror in method
